title: Eurycaulis calcarifer
taxonomy: Eurycaulis calcarifer is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia, Indonesia, and the Philippines. This evergreen tree is classified as a member of the genus Eurycaulis and is often referred to as the 'Ironwood' tree due to its extremely hard wood. The plant has a broad distribution and is found in various types of forests, including lowland and montane forests. Eurycaulis calcarifer is an important species for local communities, providing timber and other forest products.
uses: Eurycaulis calcarifer, also known as the stilt root or stilt tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, the roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful for wound healing and skin conditions.
